tossups.question: A lyric requesting protection from the “murderous rage” of Turks and these people is often removed from modern versions of the hymn “Erhalt uns, Herr, bei deinem Wort.” To open a work titled for one of these people, each voice leaps up a perfect fourth and descends in a stepwise fashion. A work titled for one of these people splits the text of the Agnus Dei into two movements. A genre named for one of these people was modified by adding an improvised vox organalis to the vox principalis to create works called organum. Legendarily, a ban on polyphony was averted by the composition of a mass titled for a man with this position, Marcellus II, by Giovanni Palestrina. For 10 points, name this position held by the namesake of a form of monophonic chant, Gregory I.
tossups.question_sanitized: A lyric requesting protection from the "murderous rage" of Turks and these people is often removed from modern versions of the hymn "Erhalt uns, Herr, bei deinem Wort." To open a work titled for one of these people, each voice leaps up a perfect fourth and descends in a stepwise fashion. A work titled for one of these people splits the text of the Agnus Dei into two movements. A genre named for one of these people was modified by adding an improvised vox organalis to the vox principalis to create works called organum. Legendarily, a ban on polyphony was averted by the composition of a mass titled for a man with this position, Marcellus II, by Giovanni Palestrina. For 10 points, name this position held by the namesake of a form of monophonic chant, Gregory I.
tossups.answer: Pope [accept bishop of Rome; accept supreme pontiff or Roman pontiff or sovereign pontiff; accept Pontifex maximus; accept Pope Marcellus Mass; accept Missa Papae Marcelli]
tossups.answer_sanitized: Pope [accept bishop of Rome; accept supreme pontiff or Roman pontiff or sovereign pontiff; accept Pontifex maximus; accept Pope Marcellus Mass; accept Missa Papae Marcelli]

packet.name: L - Florida A, Simon Fraser A, Toronto D, Waterloo A

tossups.question: An anonymous example of one of these pieces features a more than 100 note hold on its final word "caput." Another piece in this genre is named for the fact that it can be sung in either Dorian, Phrygian, Lydian, or Mixolydian. Examples of these pieces from the Franco-Flemish school include one named for the song Pange Lingua. "Parody" examples of these pieces based on the French song "L'homme Armé" were written by Johannes Ockeghem and Josquin des Prez. A piece in this genre apocryphally "saved polyphony" from being banned by the Catholic Church at the Council of Trent. For 10 points, identify this genre of choral music, exemplified by a Giovanni Pierluigi da Palestrina piece named after Pope Marcellus, which accompany church services.
tossups.answer: masses [or messe or missa or missi; accept Missa Caput; accept Missa in Cuiusvis Toni; accept Messe de Nostre Dame; accept Missa L'homme Armé; accept Pope Marcellus Mass or Missa Papae Marcelli; accept requiem mass; prompt on requiem with "is an example of what type of piece?"]
tossups.answer_sanitized: masses [or messe or missa or missi; accept Missa Caput; accept Missa in Cuiusvis Toni; accept Messe de Nostre Dame; accept Missa L'homme Arme; accept Pope Marcellus Mass or Missa Papae Marcelli; accept requiem mass; prompt on requiem with "is an example of what type of piece?"]

Another piece in this genre is named for the fact that it can be sung in either Dorian, Phrygian, Lydian, or Mixolydian. Examples of these pieces from the Franco-Flemish school include one named for the song Pange Lingua. "Parody" examples of these pieces based on the French song "L'homme Arme" were written by Johannes Ockeghem and Josquin des Prez. A piece in this genre apocryphally "saved polyphony" from being banned by the Catholic Church at the Council of Trent. For 10 points, identify this genre of choral music, exemplified by a Giovanni Pierluigi da Palestrina piece named after Pope Marcellus, which accompany church services.

bonuses.leadin: This man titles a mass whose Kyrie (“KEE-ree-ay”) section introduces the following recurring motif: “D [pause] D, D [pause] G, F, E, D.” For 10 points each:
parts: Name this pope who is the namesake of a mass by Giovanni Pierluigi da Palestrina that is erroneously credited with saving Renaissance polyphony.

In Gradus ad Parnassum, Johann Joseph Fux cited Palestrina’s style as an example of counterpoint that uses and is named for these entities. “Note against note” is the first of these entities, which are used to pedagogically conceptualize the progression of counterpoint with elements such as the cantus firmus.
Palestrina also wrote many pieces in this genre of sacred polyphonic choral music. This genre is often contrasted with the secular madrigal.
answers: Pope Marcellus II [accept Pope Marcellus Mass or Missa Papae Marcelli; accept Marcello Cervini degli Spannochi]

packet.name: H - Auburn A, Brown A, Duke B, Iowa A, and NYU A

answers_sanitized: Pope Marcellus II [accept Pope Marcellus Mass or Missa Papae Marcelli; accept Marcello Cervini degli Spannochi]

parts_sanitized: Name this pope who is the namesake of a mass by Giovanni Pierluigi da Palestrina that is erroneously credited with saving Renaissance polyphony.

In Gradus ad Parnassum, Johann Joseph Fux cited Palestrina's style as an example of counterpoint that uses and is named for these entities. "Note against note" is the first of these entities, which are used to pedagogically conceptualize the progression of counterpoint with elements such as the cantus firmus.
Palestrina also wrote many pieces in this genre of sacred polyphonic choral music. This genre is often contrasted with the secular madrigal.

bonuses.leadin: The second half of the modern version of this piece is oddly transcribed to C minor, which is now believed to be a copyist’s error. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this setting of Psalm 51 that was typically performed during Tenebrae service. This piece was banned from reproduction until a young Mozart supposedly transcribed the piece from memory after hearing it in a mass.
One of the most famous recordings of the Miserere is by a British ensemble founded by Peter Phillips named for this Renaissance British composer of Spem in Alium.

In their debut recording, the Tallis Scholars performed this ordinary mass alongside Allegri’s Miserere. The Vatican supposedly decided that polyphony was worth saving in the Council of Trent after hearing this mass.
answers_sanitized: Pope Marcellus Mass [or Missa Papae Marcelli]
bonuses.leadin_sanitized: The second half of the modern version of this piece is oddly transcribed to C minor, which is now believed to be a copyist's error. For 10 points each:
parts_sanitized: Name this setting of Psalm 51 that was typically performed during Tenebrae service. This piece was banned from reproduction until a young Mozart supposedly transcribed the piece from memory after hearing it in a mass.
One of the most famous recordings of the Miserere is by a British ensemble founded by Peter Phillips named for this Renaissance British composer of Spem in Alium.

In their debut recording, the Tallis Scholars performed this ordinary mass alongside Allegri's Miserere. The Vatican supposedly decided that polyphony was worth saving in the Council of Trent after hearing this mass.

bonuses.leadin: Pope John XXII denounced this musical practice in his Docta Sanctorum Patrum. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this musical texture whose name means “many sounds.” In it, at least two independent voices sound out together at the same time.
A session of the Council of Trent was legendarily persuaded by this composition to keep polyphony, an event commemorated by a Hans Pfitzner opera. It is named for a pontiff with a three-week reign.

This composer wrote a Miserere that was never supposed to be copied outside of the Vatican. Unfortunately for the Vatican, they didn’t account for a 14-year-old Mozart, who transcribed it anyway upon hearing it twice.
answers_sanitized: Pope Marcellus Mass [accept Missa Papae Marcelli]
bonuses.leadin_sanitized: Pope John XXII denounced this musical practice in his Docta Sanctorum Patrum. For 10 points each:
parts_sanitized: Name this musical texture whose name means "many sounds." In it, at least two independent voices sound out together at the same time.
A session of the Council of Trent was legendarily persuaded by this composition to keep polyphony, an event commemorated by a Hans Pfitzner opera. It is named for a pontiff with a three-week reign.

This composer wrote a Miserere that was never supposed to be copied outside of the Vatican. Unfortunately for the Vatican, they didn't account for a 14-year-old Mozart, who transcribed it anyway upon hearing it twice.

bonuses.leadin: This composer believed dissonance should be confined to weak beats, according to a theory advocating him by Joseph Fux, for 10 points:
parts: Name this Renaissance composer and master of counterpoint whose works include a series of motets based on the Song of Solomon, an eight part setting of the Stabat Mater, and a Missa sina nomine that may have influenced Bach’s Mass in B Minor.
Palestrina is most famous for this work, which according to legend convinced the Council of Trent not to ban polyphonic settings of sacred text.
A notable school of polyphony was lead by composers like Leonin and Perotin and was named this. This is also the name of an early polyphonic mass by Guillame de Machaut.
answers_sanitized: Pope Marcellus Mass [or Missa Papae Marcelli]
